posting.sponsorName: Joint Apprenticeship and Training Committee (JATC) for Plumbers Local Union #1
posting.applicationSummary: Obtain the application in person at the JATC Training Center, then return the completed application by hand during the return window. Recruitment is limited to 1,000 applications, issued first-come, first-served.
posting.applicationInPersonAddress: JATC for Plumbers Local Union #1 Training Center, 37-11 47th Avenue, Long Island City, NY 11101
posting.applicationHours: Applications may be picked up in person Monday through Friday, 8:00 a.m. to 3:00 p.m., during the recruitment period, excluding Saturdays and Sundays. Applications will not be available on Friday, June 19, 2026. Completed applications must be returned by hand Friday, July 17, 2026 through Thursday, July 23, 2026, excluding Saturdays and Sundays, from 9:00 a.m. to 12:30 p.m. and 1:30 p.m. to 3:00 p.m.
posting.applicationLimitDetails: Limited-application recruitment. Only 1,000 applications will be distributed, on a first-come, first-served basis, and recruitment will continue only until 1,000 applications have been issued.
posting.applicationFeeDetails: A $25 application fee and a $25 testing fee are required. Applicants must submit a $50 money order with the application; no cash or checks are accepted. A fee waiver may be requested and may be approved with verifiable proof of financial need. Fees may not result in profit to the sponsor.
posting.ageRequirement: Must be at least 18 years old at the time of entry into the apprenticeship program.
posting.educationRequirement: Must have a diploma from an accredited high school showing at least four calendar years of high school work and a transcript. In lieu of this, the committee accepts a High School Equivalency (HSE) diploma with a transcript confirming GED or TASC scores. International students must submit a state-issued HSE diploma with a transcript confirming TASC or GED scores.
posting.drugTestRequirement: Must pass a drug test, at the sponsor's expense, after selection and before enrollment.
posting.classRequirement: Must take a TABE assessment test.
allRequirements: Must be at least 18 years old at the time of entry into the apprenticeship program.
Must submit a diploma from an accredited high school reflecting a minimum of four calendar years of high school work in an accredited school system with transcript.

In lieu of a four-year high school diploma, the committee will accept a High School Equivalency (HSE) diploma issued by the state department of education of the state in which the test was taken, with a transcript confirming GED or TASC scores.

International students must submit a state-issued High School Equivalency (HSE) diploma issued by the state department of education in the state in which the test was taken, with a transcript confirming TASC or GED scores.
Must take and pass a drug test, at the expense of the sponsor, after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must be legally able to work in the USA and show proof after selection and prior to enrollment in apprenticeship.
Must appear for a personal interview.
Must take a TABE assessment test.
Must bring a form of positive proof of identification when obtaining the application.
Only one application per person will be issued until the supply of 1,000 applications is exhausted.
Applications must be obtained in person at the JATC Training Center.
Completed applications must be returned by hand, not by mail.
Applications must be fully completed and submitted with all relevant related documentation.
All applications must be received by no later than Thursday, July 23, 2026.
posting.requirementsSummary: Applicants must be at least 18, have the required high school diploma or HSE, pass a drug test, be legally able to work in the U.S., and complete an interview and TABE test. Applications are limited to 1,000 and must be obtained and returned in person.
posting.contactSummary: Contact the JATC for Plumbers Local Union #1 at (718) 752-9630 for further information.
